description_long: This bungalow features a traditional East Asian architectural style, with a prominent tiled roof that curves upward at the corners and decorative finials at each end. The front facade includes a small covered porch with two lanterns flanking the entrance, and ornamental plants on either side of the steps. The walls are adorned with symmetrical windows, and the roof tiles are meticulously detailed, giving the structure a sense of authenticity and craftsmanship. The side views reveal the depth of the porch and the overhanging eaves, while the rear view shows three evenly spaced windows. The building appears to be constructed primarily from masonry or stucco, with wooden elements for the roof and porch. The overall impression is of a compact, single-story residence with cultural and historical character.
description: A compact, single-story bungalow with a traditional East Asian tiled roof, symmetrical windows, and a small covered porch with lanterns and plants.
description_view_0: Side view showing the depth of the porch, overhanging eaves, and a lantern hanging near the entrance.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the main entrance, two lanterns, ornamental plants, and symmetrical windows.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring the first, with visible porch depth and lantern, and partial view of the plants.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying three evenly spaced windows and the full width of the tiled roof.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with evenly spaced rear windows
